description: Phase 2E — Close the loop: round-trip fidelity testing, drift detection, golden-corpus fixtures. Validates that every artifact type is faithfully re-ingestable. See vaults/llm-wiki/wiki/concepts/close-the-loop-testing.md for the design.

userStories.title: Ensure source-hash sidecars across all generate-* handlers
userStories.description: As the verify skill, I need every artifact to carry a deterministic source-hash so I can detect drift and recompute the baseline for round-trip tests.
acceptanceCriteria: Audit every .claude/skills/generate-*/SKILL.md — confirm .meta.yaml sidecar includes source-hash computed over the concatenated source wiki page contents (normalised newlines, stripped trailing whitespace)
Extract a single shared helper (bash or python) at .claude/skills/generate/lib/source-hash.sh that all handlers call — DRY compliance
Unit tests: given a fixture of 3 markdown files, helper produces a stable hash; a whitespace-only change does not change the hash; a content change does
Backfill any generate-* handler missing source-hash
Document the hashing algorithm in reference/artifacts.md
userStories.notes: Deterministic hashing is the foundation of everything else in 2E. Get this right first. — Done: audited all 10 generate-* SKILL.md files (book, pdf, slides, mindmap, infographic, podcast, video, quiz, flashcards, app) — every one already calls .claude/skills/generate/lib/source-hash.sh and emits `source-hash:` in the .meta.yaml sidecar. No backfill needed. Added unit test suite at .claude/skills/generate/lib/tests/test-source-hash.sh covering: baseline digest length, idempotence, order-independence, stdin/argv parity, whitespace-only invariance (trailing spaces, blank-line runs, CRLF, EOF blanks), internal-whitespace sensitivity (semantic), content sensitivity, missing-file exit, empty-input exit — 9/9 passing. Documented the algorithm in sites/docs/src/content/docs/reference/artifacts.md including the internal-whitespace nuance and a run-the-tests pointer.

userStories.title: Implement /verify-artifact skill (round-trip fidelity test)
userStories.description: As a user, I want `/verify-artifact <type> <topic>` to generate the artifact, re-ingest it into a scratch vault, diff against the original wiki pages, and report a fidelity score.
acceptanceCriteria: Create .claude/skills/verify-artifact/SKILL.md

Pipeline: (1) run /generate <type> <topic> → artifact A, (2) create throwaway scratch vault under /tmp/, (3) run the matching /ingest handler on A → pages P', (4) compare P' against original pages P, (5) emit fidelity report, (6) clean up scratch vault
Report includes: coverage percent, concept-set Jaccard similarity, LLM-judge fact-level diff (optional, flag-gated)
Expected-recoverability targets per artifact type encoded in the skill (book 0.85, slides 0.35, quiz 0.40, etc. — see close-the-loop-testing.md)
Exit code non-zero if fidelity below target — usable in CI
Supports --keep-scratch flag to preserve the scratch vault for manual inspection
Handles all artifact→ingest mappings: PDF→ingest-pdf, MP3→whisper→ingest-text, MP4→whisper+OCR→ingest-text, quiz.json→ingest-text, etc.
userStories.notes: Whisper for audio/video transcription is lazy-installed here. May already exist in ingest-video; reuse. — Done: .claude/skills/verify-artifact/SKILL.md implements the 8-step pipeline (select source pages → /generate → scratch vault under /tmp → inverse-path re-ingest → diff → score → report → cleanup). Inverse path prefers re-renderable source sidecars (.script.md for podcast/video, .scenes.json for video, .questions.json for quiz, .cards.csv for flashcards, src/data.json for app) over PDF/whisper/OCR — cheap, deterministic, no external transcription dep for the common case. Python scoring heredoc computes: (a) per-page coverage (concept overlap ≥0.5 threshold), (b) corpus-level Jaccard similarity over extracted concept sets, (c) weighted fidelity = 0.6*coverage + 0.4*jaccard. Per-type recoverability targets from close-the-loop-testing.md encoded in the skill (book 0.85, pdf 0.85, podcast 0.75, video 0.60, mindmap 0.50, flashcards 0.40, quiz 0.40, slides 0.35, app 0.25, infographic 0.25). Flags: --target (override), --llm-judge (flag-gated expensive tier), --keep-scratch (debug), --from (verify existing artifact without regenerating). Exit 0 pass / 1 fidelity fail / 2+ infra error — CI-usable. user-invocable: true (first user-facing skill alongside /generate). MP4 binary-only path deferred to future iteration; documented limitation in SKILL.md.

userStories.title: Extend /lint with --artifacts mode for drift detection
userStories.description: As a user, I want `/lint --artifacts` to flag artifacts whose source wiki has drifted since generation, without doing a full re-verify pass.
acceptanceCriteria: Modify .claude/skills/lint/SKILL.md to accept --artifacts flag
In --artifacts mode: walk <vault>/artifacts/ for .meta.yaml sidecars, recompute source-hash from current wiki pages, flag mismatches
Report format: artifact path, generated-at timestamp, old hash, new hash, drift-age (git log commits on source pages since generated-at)
Optional --verify flag chains into /verify-artifact for flagged artifacts
Existing /lint behaviour unchanged when --artifacts not passed

userStories.title: Create golden-corpus fixture + advisory CI check
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want a known-good set of wiki pages whose artifact generation is tracked over time so template regressions are caught.
acceptanceCriteria: Create golden-corpus/ directory at repo root (committed, not gitignored) with 3–5 small example wiki pages (cleanly concept-focused)
Add .github/workflows/golden-corpus.yml that on push runs /generate for each artifact type against the corpus and uploads outputs as CI artifacts
CI step runs /verify-artifact for each type and fails advisory (continue-on-error: true for initial rollout — per close-the-loop-testing.md non-goals)
Document golden-corpus in sites/docs/src/content/docs/reference/
Include a small README in golden-corpus/ explaining the intent and how to add new fixtures
userStories.notes: Advisory first. Hard-fail in CI can come in 2E.2 once scores stabilise.

userStories.title: Document Phase 2E in sites/docs
userStories.description: As a user, I want docs for /verify-artifact, /lint --artifacts, the fidelity-score system, and golden-corpus.
acceptanceCriteria: Add sites/docs/src/content/docs/features/verify-artifact.md with the round-trip diagram from close-the-loop-testing.md
Update sites/docs/src/content/docs/features/lint.md with --artifacts mode
Add sites/docs/src/content/docs/reference/fidelity-scoring.md explaining the three-tier scoring and per-artifact targets
Update commands.md reference
Cross-link from artifact-generation-vision page in Starlight (if promoted) or from research/roadmap.md
Mermaid diagrams per doc-standards
